message:
feat(harness,observability): lifecycle state tracking in VirtualNetworkRuntime, obligation tracker time fallback

Network harness lifecycle tracking:
- PendingResultsMap entries now carry a PendingResultEntry struct with
  both the oneshot sender and a RemoteTaskState, replacing the bare
  sender. This allows the harness to track per-task lifecycle state
  (Pending → Running → Completed/Cancelled/Failed) in sync with the
  new RemoteRuntime::observe_task_state/clear_task_state trait methods.

- VirtualNetworkRuntime implements observe_task_state() and
  clear_task_state(), enabling RemoteHandle::state() to query live
  lifecycle state during harness-driven tests.

- SimNode::handle_spawn_ack() is no longer a no-op: it now transitions
  pending tasks to Running on SpawnAckStatus::Accepted, and delivers
  SpawnRejected errors on SpawnAckStatus::Rejected—closing the loop
  so origin-side handles see rejection immediately rather than hanging.

- SimNode::handle_result() and DistributedHarness::route_message() now
  update lifecycle state before extracting the oneshot sender, ensuring
  observe_task_state() returns the correct terminal state even if
  polled between delivery and consumption.

- New test: rejected_spawn_ack_fails_pending_remote_handle verifies the
  full rejection path through the harness.

Obligation tracker time source fallback:
- ObligationTracker::current_time() now falls back to RuntimeState::now
  (the logical runtime clock) when no timer driver is installed, instead
  of returning Time::ZERO. This fixes obligation age computation in
  timerless test runtimes—previously all obligations showed age=0,
  making leak detection and age warnings non-functional in tests.

- Two new tests verify the fallback (tracker_uses_runtime_logical_time_
  without_timer_driver) and the preference for the timer driver when
  present (tracker_prefers_timer_driver_when_available).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
